howItWorks.intro: This vertical jump calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Vertical Jump
rules.explanation: The difference between your highest jump touch and your standing reach.

rules.name: Peak Power (Sayers)
rules.explanation: The Sayers equation estimates peak anaerobic power from vertical jump height and body mass.

howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is a good vertical jump?
faq.answer: For adult males, 20-24 inches is above average, 24-28 is very good, and 28+ is excellent (elite athletes). For females, 16-20 inches is above average and 24+ is excellent. NBA players average around 28-34 inches.

faq.question: How can I increase my vertical jump?
faq.answer: Focus on plyometrics (box jumps, depth jumps), heavy squats, and Bulgarian split squats. Most people can add 4-8 inches over 8-12 weeks of dedicated training. Losing excess body weight also directly improves jump height.

faq.question: Is the Vertical Jump Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard fitness and training formulas trusted by athletes and fitness enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What standing reach should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate standing reach value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Vertical Jump Calculator?
sections.body: The Vertical Jump Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your standing reach, jump touch height, body weight, you get instant results including vertical jump, rating, estimated peak power. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting vertical jump right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your standing reach and need to find the right vertical jump. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Standing Reach (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your standing reach — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: jump touch height, body weight, gender. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your vertical jump, rating, estimated peak power. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your standing reach before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both vertical jump and rating — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your training.
If you're unsure about your gender, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
